intro: You are logged in as Tenant Admin. This role owns the full hospital platform. The sidebar groups modules by care phase with clear labels — no numbered prefixes. Create and Save buttons appear on every module desk. All tables load live rows from PostgreSQL — eight demo patients, lab orders, billing, and records across thirty-six modules.

routes.say: The admin dashboard shows every KPI your role can access — patient counts, appointments, lab queue, and revenue — each number queried from the database.
Patients lists all registered patients with search, pagination, and export. Demo tenant has eight patients with MRNs starting DEMO.
Appointments shows booked slots, queue tokens, and telemedicine visits pulled from the appointments table.
Billing displays invoices and payment status. Demo data includes multiple issued invoices linked to real patients.
Laboratory shows CBC orders in various workflow states — ordered, collected, verified — all from lab_orders. Lab techs enter results; doctors can order new tests from this screen.
Inventory and procurement desk with Create and Save on every submodule tab — demo stock and purchase records loaded from module_records.
Reports runs analytics definitions stored in the database and returns detail rows you can export.
Module map is the full catalog of thirty-six integrated modules with work-item counts from module_records.

intro: You are logged in as Doctor. Notice the sidebar — only clinical screens appear. Billing, administration, inventory, and operations modules are hidden. You cannot navigate to them even by URL.

routes.say: Doctor dashboard shows clinical KPIs only — patients, appointments, open encounters, and lab orders relevant to your practice.
Read-only patient list for chart review. Search and export work on live patient rows.
Encounters lists OPD visits with chief complaint, vitals, and clinical notes stored in the database.
Telemedicine shows completed and scheduled video sessions with room IDs from telemedicine_sessions.
Doctors can view lab orders, order new tests, and track results. Orders reference master test codes like CBC from lab_tests.
Pharmacy queue shows dispense requests — read access for doctors to track prescriptions.

intro: You are logged in as Nurse. The menu is limited to nursing and inpatient care. There is no billing, no finance, no pharmacy admin, and no tenant settings beyond MFA.

routes.say: Nurse dashboard KPIs focus on patients, encounters, nursing tasks, and inpatient census — not revenue or claims.
Patient list for ward rounds and vitals capture.
Active and closed encounters where nurses document vitals and progress notes.
Nursing tasks desk shows vitals checks, medication administration, and wound care items. Use Create task and Save to add new nursing work from nursing_tasks.
Inpatient admissions with bed allocation — demo patient Vikram Singh is admitted to ward GEN.

intro: You are logged in as Receptionist. Front-office screens only — register patients, book appointments, manage emergency triage queue, and view providers. Clinical order entry and billing write access are not in your menu.

routes.say: Reception dashboard highlights appointment volume and patient registrations.
Full patient registration and CRM — create and edit patient demographics.
Appointment booking with queue tokens and check-in status from the database.
Emergency triage board with ESI levels and disposition — four demo triage cases loaded.
Provider directory with specialties and consultation schedules.

intro: You are logged in as Billing Staff. Finance and revenue screens only. No clinical modules, no nursing, no laboratory order entry appear in your sidebar.

routes.say: Billing desk with invoices, estimates, and UPI payments — demo invoices INV-DEMO series in the database.
Insurance claims in draft through paid status with payer codes STAR and ICICI from insurance_payers master.
Revenue cycle view combining claims, invoices, and payer contracts.
Financial reports — revenue dashboard and audit trail exports.

intro: You are logged in as Pharmacist. Your workspace opens on the pharmacy dispense queue. Only pharmacy and patient lookup are available.

routes.say: Pharmacy dispense queue with medicines from masters — Paracetamol and Amoxicillin stock levels in medicines table.
Patient lookup to verify prescription against the correct MRN before dispensing.

intro: You are logged in as Lab Technician. You land directly on the laboratory workbench. No other hospital modules appear in navigation.

routes.say: Lab order worklist with workflow buttons driven by API — sample collection, result entry, verification. Eight demo orders in PostgreSQL.
Patient lookup to match samples to MRN before processing.

intro: You are logged in as Radiologist. Imaging orders and reporting only. Study codes come from radiology tariffs and existing orders — not hardcoded lists.

routes.say: Imaging orders for chest X-ray, ultrasound, CT, and MRI with report dictation. Status workflow from ordered to reported.
Patient context for imaging orders — name and MRN from patients table.

intro: You are logged in as Patient portal user Rajesh Kumar. The entire hospital admin UI is hidden. Only self-service portal screens are available.

routes.say: Patient portal shows your appointments, bills, telemedicine join links, and visit summaries — filtered to your patient record by email patient@demo.sumaya.
